verdict_reason: The verse is the chapter's first place where Advaita and Vishishtadvaita commentaries diverge sharply — Shankara universalises asat (everything changeful is asat); Ramanuja localises it (only the body is asat). The engineering layer correctly does not pick a side. It anchors on the operational consequence both bhasyas share (the seers see the conclusion of both — and act accordingly), and the source pack's disagreements field surfaces the divergence honestly. The technical term tattva-darśin is preserved verbatim. The risk a sloppier writer would have run — flattening sat / asat into a general productivity slogan ('focus on what's real') — is explicitly avoided; the analog stays on a verse-specific operational move (audit the signal source). No tradition is misrepresented or smuggled in. PASS.
